data.description:
## Story F: End-to-end validation — Ruby project fixture proves the full chain

### Description
**What**: Add a test fixture and end-to-end test that (1) runs onboarding on a Ruby project skeleton, (2) asserts `commands.test_runner = bundle exec rspec` in the generated `dso-config.conf`, (3) exercises the test-gate recording script against a `.rb` source file, and (4) asserts the result is recorded via RSpec (not pytest). This is the epic's explicit validation SC.
**Why**: Without this end-to-end fixture, each story passes unit tests in isolation while the integrated polyglot path is never exercised in CI. The epic's Validation SC demands this.
**Scope**:
- IN: Ruby project fixture (minimal Gemfile + one RSpec test), E2E test script, CI wiring (opt-in similar to existing `RUN_REAL_URL_E2E`)
- OUT: Node/JS equivalent fixture (valid follow-on — not in this epic per SC scope), real-project integration testing (this is a controlled fixture)

### Done Definitions
- When this story is complete, running `tests/scripts/test-polyglot-ruby-e2e.sh` (or equivalent) passes without warnings
  ← Satisfies: "Validation: run onboarding on a Ruby project; commands.test_runner = bundle exec rspec written to dso-config.conf; record-test-status.sh against a .rb file records result using RSpec, not pytest"
- When this story is complete, INSTALL.md has a "Per-stack test runner setup" section with notes on Jest, RSpec, and pytest optional dependencies
  ← Satisfies: "INSTALL.md updated with per-stack test runner optional dependency notes (Jest, RSpec, pytest)"

### Considerations
- [Testing] RSpec and bundle are assumed available in the CI environment; fixture test should gate on tool availability and skip (not fail) if absent — document the env requirement in INSTALL.md
- [Reliability] Fixture must not pollute the parent repo's `.tickets-tracker/` — use an isolated temp-repo pattern consistent with other E2E tests
- [Testing] Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic
## Escalation Policy
**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
data.title: As a maintainer, a Ruby project end-to-end fixture proves the full polyglot test-gate chain
